rbi_monetary_policy.description: RBI Annual Report data, guidelines, payment systems, CBDC, monetary policy, inflation targets, FSR, DPI
hot_subtopics: RBI Annual Report statistics (GNPA, NPA ratios, GDP forecasts)
RBI Payment Systems (NEFT, RTGS, UPI, CBDC/Digital Rupee)
Monetary Policy Committee decisions, repo rate, inflation target (6% upper tolerance, 3 quarters)
RBI Initiatives and dashboards (Antardrishti, Money Kumar mascot)
Digital Payments Index (DPI) - Payment Performance has highest weight
rbi_monetary_policy.year_trend: Consistently highest weight in all 3 years. 2025 shifted toward static RBI knowledge over current circulars.

banking_financial_sector.description: UCBs, NBFCs, SFBs, SEBI regulations, IRDAI, cooperative banks, payment banks, ARCs, SARFAESI
hot_subtopics: UCB tier classification and exposure limits
Call Money / Notice Money / Term Money markets
RBI committees on customer service (Talwar, Goiporia, Damodaran)
banking_financial_sector.year_trend: Growing in 2024-25. More static banking knowledge being tested, not just current affairs.

government_schemes_budget.description: Union Budget provisions, central government schemes, GST, tax policy, infrastructure projects
hot_subtopics: Union Budget key figures (surcharge rates, GST thresholds, subsidies)
Housing schemes (PMAY-U with specific subsidy amounts)
Small savings schemes (Senior Citizen > Sukanya > NSC returns)
government_schemes_budget.year_trend: Stable across years. Budget-linked questions peak in exam year after budget announcement.

international_affairs_organizations.description: G20, BRICS, SCO, ASEAN, BIMSTEC, bilateral agreements, FTAs, international appointments, global bodies
hot_subtopics: India's FTAs (signed vs not signed - Brazil not signed)
Bilateral agreements and declarations (Atlantic Declaration etc.)
Membership questions (India NOT member of OECD)
international_affairs_organizations.year_trend: High in 2023 (India G20 presidency). Shifted to bilateral/FTA questions in 2024-25.

economy_trade_data.description: GDP data, trade figures, WPI, exports, global growth contribution, Twenty Point Programme
hot_subtopics: India GDP growth forecasts (SPF by RBI)
India's global growth contribution (12% of global growth)
Financial Saving of Household Sector (shares & debentures highest share)
Commercial banks as govt securities holders (37.5% at end-March 2023)
economy_trade_data.year_trend: Number-heavy questions. Exact figures matter. RBI Annual Report is the primary source.

awards_rankings_appointments.description: International awards, India rankings, key domestic and global appointments
hot_subtopics: NIRF rankings (IISc, IIT Madras, St Stephens)
Global Gender Gap Index top countries
CAG of India's external auditor roles in UN bodies
awards_rankings_appointments.year_trend: Consistent across years. Indian-origin international awardees is a reliable topic.

sports.description: Olympics, cricket, tennis, badminton, hockey. Mostly recent events + historical milestones.
hot_subtopics: Olympic medals and firsts (Abhinav Bindra - first individual gold, 2008 Beijing)
ICC Cricket World Cup historical facts
sports.year_trend: Olympics year (2024) had more sports questions. Usually 3-5 questions per paper.

environment_geography.description: Climate phenomena, biodiversity, physical geography, UNESCO sites, conservation
hot_subtopics: El Nino / Indian Ocean Dipole relationship
Top mangrove cover states (West Bengal, Gujarat, Andaman)
UNESCO World Heritage Sites in India
Mountain peaks by country (Anamudi = India, Everest = Nepal)
environment_geography.year_trend: Relatively stable. Physical geography increased in 2025 paper.

science_technology.description: ISRO missions, space news, AI/fintech policy, digital initiatives
hot_subtopics: ISRO facts (first Chairman Dr Vikram Sarabhai, HQ Bengaluru, est. 1969)
TRAI digital consent (DCA - Digital Consent Acquisition)
science_technology.year_trend: Increasing in 2024-25. AI and fintech questions growing.

static_miscellaneous.description: HQ locations, founding years, river-continent matching, historical GK
hot_subtopics: Central bank HQ locations (ECB = Frankfurt, est. 1998)
Commemorative coins (75 Rupee coin = no silver)
static_miscellaneous.year_trend: Appears in 2025 more than earlier years. Treat as bonus, not core focus.

fill_in_the_blank.description: One or two blanks in a factual sentence. Tests exact numbers, names, dates.
fill_in_the_blank.example_pattern: The Upper Tolerance level of Inflation is ___ and in case of breach for ___ quarters the report has to be submitted by RBI.
fill_in_the_blank.writing_instructions: Include exact figures in the correct option. Wrong options should be plausibly close numbers. Never use round numbers that are too obvious.
fill_in_the_blank.trap_pattern: Options differ by small margins (e.g. 6.0% vs 6.2% vs 5.9%). Student must know precise figure.

direct_factual.description: Which country/person/organization did X. Short, direct.
direct_factual.example_pattern: Which country has filed maximum number of GenAI patents as per WIPO?
direct_factual.writing_instructions: Question stem should reference a specific report/event/date. Options should include plausible distractors from the same category.
direct_factual.trap_pattern: Include USA/UK/India as distractors even when answer is China or less expected country.

statement_correct_incorrect.description: 2-3 statements given. Find which is/are correct or incorrect.
statement_correct_incorrect.example_pattern: Consider the following statements about X: 1. [stmt] 2. [stmt] 3. [stmt]. Which is/are correct?
statement_correct_incorrect.writing_instructions: Make 2 statements correct and 1 wrong OR 1 correct and 2 wrong. The wrong statement should have a subtly wrong number or fact. Options: A. Only 1, B. Only 2, C. 1 and 2, D. 2 and 3, E. All of the above
statement_correct_incorrect.trap_pattern: Option E (All of the above) is correct more often than students expect.

not_odd_one_out.description: Which is NOT a member/feature/part of X. Tests exclusions.
not_odd_one_out.example_pattern: India is NOT a member of which of the following?
not_odd_one_out.writing_instructions: Use NOT in capitals or bold. The correct answer (the odd one out) should be a commonly confused entity. E.g. OECD (India not member), Brazil (India FTA not signed).
not_odd_one_out.trap_pattern: Include 'None of the above' as option E which is wrong — all others are genuinely members/features.

multiple_statements_select.description: 4-5 items listed as 1,2,3,4. Select correct combination.
multiple_statements_select.example_pattern: Which of the following States are among top 3 with highest mangrove cover? 1. WB 2. Gujarat 3. A&N 4. TN — A.1,2&3 B.Only 2&3...
multiple_statements_select.writing_instructions: Options should be combinations like: A.1,2&3  B.2,3&4  C.1&3 only  D.3&4 only  E.All of the above. Make correct answer include 3 items usually.
multiple_statements_select.trap_pattern: 'All of the above' or the largest combination is frequently correct.

match_the_following.description: List I matched with List II. Tests association of pairs.
match_the_following.example_pattern: Match the tiers of UCBs (List I) with loan limits (List II). Select correct code.
match_the_following.writing_instructions: Always 4 pairs. Options show 4 codes like A. 1-D,2-A,3-B,4-C. Make one option have 2 pairs swapped. Option E should be 'None of the above'.
match_the_following.trap_pattern: One of the middle options has 3 correct and 1 wrong — student must check all 4.

critical_format_rules.options_count: ALWAYS 5 options (A, B, C, D, E). Never 4. This is non-negotiable for RBI Grade B.
question_stem_patterns: Start with 'Recently,' for current affairs questions
Reference specific reports (RBI Annual Report 2023-24, FSR, SPF)
Include year in the question stem when relevant
Use 'Which of the following' as the most common opener
Long context paragraph before the actual question for complex topics
critical_format_rules.number_questions_rule: For fill-in-the-blank with numbers, options must be close to each other. Never obvious. E.g. 5.7%, 5.9%, 6.0%, 6.1%, 6.2% — not 5%, 10%, 15%, 20%, 25%.
critical_format_rules.distractor_quality: Wrong options must be plausible entities from the same category. E.g. if answer is NABARD, distractors should be SIDBI, NHB, MUDRA, RBI — not random unrelated bodies.

high_priority_topics_for_current_year.note: These are perennial high-weight areas that will ALWAYS have questions regardless of year
must_prepare: RBI Annual Report key statistics for the latest year
Latest FSR (Financial Stability Report) data — GNPA ratio trend
Recent RBI circulars on UCBs, NBFCs, HFCs
Monetary Policy Committee decisions — current repo rate, inflation band
Recent India FTAs and bilateral deals
India's position in major international indices
Recent major government schemes with specific outlay amounts

pipeline_prompt_instructions.usage: Paste this block into the Groq/Gemini system prompt in daily_runner.py to make generated questions match actual exam pattern.
pipeline_prompt_instructions.inject_into_prompt:
Generate exactly 15 MCQs strictly following RBI Grade B Phase 1 GA exam pattern:

TOPIC DISTRIBUTION (generate this many questions per topic):
- RBI & Monetary Policy: 4 questions
- Banking & Financial Sector: 3 questions
- Government Schemes & Budget: 2 questions
- International Affairs & Organizations: 2 questions
- Economy & Trade Data: 2 questions
- Awards/Rankings/Appointments: 1 question
- Sports/Environment/Science: 1 question

QUESTION STYLE DISTRIBUTION:
- Fill in the blank (exact numbers/names): 4-5 questions
- Direct factual (who/what/where): 3-4 questions
- Statement correct/incorrect (2-3 statements): 3 questions
- NOT/odd one out: 2 questions
- Multiple statements select combination: 2 questions
- Match the following: 1 question

CRITICAL FORMAT RULES:
1. ALWAYS use 5 options: A, B, C, D, E — NEVER 4
2. Option E must often be: None of the above / All of the above / Both A & B / 1,2 and 3
3. For number-based fill-in-blank: options must be close to each other (e.g. 5.7%, 5.9%, 6.0%, 6.1%, 6.2%)
4. Start current affairs questions with 'Recently,'
5. Reference specific reports (RBI Annual Report, FSR, Union Budget) in question stems
6. Wrong options must be plausible entities from the same category
7. Format strictly as:
Q[N]. [Question text]
A. [option] B. [option] C. [option] D. [option] E. [option]
Answer: [letter]

Base all questions strictly on the content provided for this month. Do not invent facts.
